title: Catalyst molding for hydrogen peroxide activation
abstract: The present invention relates to a catalyst molding for hydrogen peroxide activation, wherein the catalyst molding comprises a titanosilicate catalyst for hydrogen peroxide activation, said catalyst comprising Ti, Si, and O, wherein the catalyst comprised in the catalyst molding displays a water adsorption (W), a concentration (C) of bridging Âµ 2 Î· 2 -peroxo species per Ti in the H 2 O 2 -activated catalyst as determined by quantitative 17 O NMR spectroscopy, and a specific activation factor (A) according to formula (I), wherein in accordance with formula (I), the activation factor is the multiplication product of the water adsorption and the concentration of bridging Âµ 2 Î· 2 -peroxo species per Ti in the H 2 O 2 -activated catalyst: A = W x C (I). The present invention further relates to a method for the preparation of a catalyst molding and a catalyst molding obtainable or obtained by said method. Yet further, the present invention relates to a process for the activation of hydrogen peroxide, and to the use of the inventive catalyst molding in a reaction involving one or more of C-O bond formation, C-C bond formation and C-C bond conversion.
claims: Claims Claims 1 . A catalyst molding for hydrogen peroxide activation, wherein the catalyst molding comprises a catalyst for hydrogen peroxide activation, said catalyst comprising Ti, Si, and O, wherein the catalyst comprised in the catalyst molding displays a water adsorption (W), a concentration (C) of bridging p 2 q 2 -peroxo species per Ti in the H2O2-activated catalyst as determined by quantitative 17 O NMR spectroscopy, and an activation factor (A) according to formula I, wherein the activation factor is in the range of from 10 to 75 mmol/mol; wherein in accordance with formula I, the activation factor is the multiplication product of the water adsorption and the concentration of bridging p 2 q 2 -peroxo species per Ti in the H2O2-activated catalyst: A = W x C (I). 2. The catalyst molding of claim 1 , wherein the catalyst molding further comprises one or more oxidic binders. 3. The catalyst molding of claim 2, wherein the one or more oxidic binders comprise one or more sources of a metal oxide and/or of a metalloid oxide selected from the group consisting of silica, alumina, titania, zirconia, lanthana, magnesia, and mixtures and/or mixed oxides of two or more thereof. 4. The catalyst molding of claim 2 or 3, wherein the catalyst molding comprises the one or more oxidic binders, calculated as the oxide, in an amount in the range of from 1 to 90 wt.- %, based on the weight of the catalyst molding. 5. The catalyst molding of any one of claims 2 to 4, wherein from 95 to 100 weight-% of the catalyst molding consist of the catalyst and the one or more oxidic binders. 6. The catalyst molding of any one of claims 1 to 5, wherein the catalyst molding exhibits a water adsorption in the range of from 1 to 15 wt.-%. 7. The catalyst molding of any one of claims 1 to 6, wherein the catalyst molding exhibits a BET specific surface area in the range of from 50 to 700 m 2 /g. 8. The catalyst molding of any one of claims 1 to 7, wherein the catalyst molding exhibits a total pore volume in the range of from 0.2 to 1.5 g/ml. 9. The catalyst molding of any one of claims 1 to 8, wherein the catalyst molding exhibits an average crush strength in the range of from 3 to 30 N. 10. The catalyst molding of any one of claims 2 to 9, wherein from 95 to 100 weight-% of the one or more oxidic binders comprised in the molding consist of Si and O. 11 . The catalyst molding of any one of claims 1 to 10, wherein the catalyst molding has a sodium content, calculated as Na2O, in the range of from 0 to 0.5 weight-% based on the weight of the catalyst molding. 12. The catalyst molding of any one of claims 1 to 11 , wherein the catalyst molding exhibits an acid site density in the range of from 1 to 1000 pmol/g at a temperature below 250 Â°C, determined according to Reference Example 1 .9. 13. The catalyst molding of any one of claims 1 to 12, wherein the catalyst molding is in the form of a strand. 14. The catalyst molding of any one of claims 1 to 13, wherein the catalyst molding is an extrudate. 15. Use of the catalyst molding according to any one of claims 1 to 14 as a catalyst and/or catalyst component in a reaction involving one or more of C-0 bond formation, C-C bond formation and C-C bond conversion.
